source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane, Diamond Ocean Blackhawk

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 82

actions_taken: Daily reports, pictures, and witness statements collected
Diamond investigation identified worn hydraulic control valve
Follow-up action to identify new visualization method for riser capture device engagement
event.summary: On December 3, 2021, at approximately 13:00 hours, the riser elevation crane on the Diamond Ocean Blackhawk drillship dropped one end of a riser joint into the riser holding area during drilling operations at MC 82. The incident resulted in damage to the aft stairway and handrail. Investigation revealed that the crane's hydraulic flow control valve had become worn and stuck in the fully engaged position, preventing proper indication of latch status to the crane operator. The evening crew flagger was new to the position and had not been trained on latch verification procedures.
recommendations: Identify a new way of visualizing riser capture devices are fully engaged to replace unreliable black indicator marks
Ensure Site-Specific Procedures (SSP) and Job Safety Analysis (JSA) encompass all steps required for riser movement operations
Implement mandatory latch verification training for personnel involved in riser handling operations

threats.name: Hydraulic control valve wear and sticking
Improper riser joint engagement in capture device

consequences.name: Riser joint dropped into riser hold area
Structural damage to stairway and handrails
Potential personnel injury from falling object

controls.name: Hydraulic flow control valve with latch indication
controls.barrier_role: Provides indication to crane operator that proper latch has been achieved before lifting
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineered control to prevent lifting of improperly latched riser

supporting_text: the crane has a hydraulic control valve designed to indicate to a proper latch has been made and it is safe to lift the riser
the elevation crane hydraulic flow control valve and its associated roller had become worn, and the switch was always engaged, indicating a complete latch regardless of hook position
the crane operator did not get an indication of the failure

controls.name: Riser elevation crane capture device with over-center cylinder position
controls.barrier_role: Mechanical latch mechanism designed to securely engage riser joints
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical barrier to prevent riser disengagement during lifting

supporting_text: when the latch was fully engaged, an over-center measurement of 8.5 mm was verified
If the latch is not fully engaged, it will not move a hydraulic flow control valve to allow the system to pick up weight

controls.name: Visual latch verification using black indicator marks on hook
controls.barrier_role: Secondary verification method to confirm proper latch engagement before lifting
controls.lod_basis: Secondary administrative control to detect improper latch before load is lifted

supporting_text: the flagger involved in the task was not properly trained and did not know what verification steps were necessary
the capture device hook black indicators was not a reliable way of demonstrating a proper latch
The difference between a good latch and one that was not complete would not be easy to detect by a person

controls.name: Site-Specific Procedure (SSP) for riser movement operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative procedure defining steps and safety requirements for moving riser from hold to cart
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure all hazards and required steps are identified and communicated

supporting_text: The Site-Specific Procedure (SSP) and Job Safety Analysis (JSA) used for this operation did not cover all steps required to move riser
The Site-Specific Procedure (SSP) being used during this operation did not encompass the activities/steps required to move the riser from the riser hold to the riser cart

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for riser movement operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative hazard identification and control measure documentation for riser movement
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify and communicate hazards and required controls

supporting_text: The JSA did not cover the hazards associated with moving the riser from the hold to the cart

controls.name: Personnel training on latch verification procedures
controls.barrier_role: Training to ensure personnel understand and can perform latch verification steps
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure personnel competence in critical safety tasks

supporting_text: the flagger involved in the task was new to the position and with no prior experience completing this task
he was not made aware he was to conduct latch verification, or that there were black indication marks on the hook to show whether a full latch has occurred
The flagger involved in the task was not properly trained and did not know what verification steps were necessary

controls.name: Automated riser elevation system with manual verification backup
controls.barrier_role: Automated system with personnel verification to ensure proper riser handling
controls.lod_basis: Engineered automation with administrative backup based on lessons from previous incidents

supporting_text: when running riser from the riser hold, there is an automated system to elevate the riser joints from the riser hold to the riser cart, and then to the drill floor

The company learned through previous incidents that the automation can fail and additional barriers (i.e., personnel verification of equipment working as designed) should be in place to conduct these operations

controls.name: Crew handover and communication procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative procedure to ensure continuity of safety-critical information during shift changes
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure critical operational information is communicated between shifts

supporting_text: At approximately 1200 hours, Diamond reports the morning rig crew handover occurred and started the riser move operation using the same elevation crane
the evening crewmember designated as the flagger and new to the position and with no prior experience completing this task

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
